_meta.session_mandate: 9-hour blueprint refinement — exhaustive planning for osm pr-split production-readiness
_meta.directive: Produce a COMPREHENSIVE, exhaustive, flat, individually-committable task list. NO priorities. NO effort estimates. EVERY task must be completed. Iteratively refine this document for the full session duration.
three_bodies: Body 1 (VERIFY): Critical terminal muxing + PTY-based async verify — interactive, observable, user-interruptible
Body 2 (UI): Sweeping UI/UX audit and fix across all 7 wizard screens
Body 3 (EXPAND): Open-ended expansion — correctness, testing, documentation, polish

codebase_facts.async_runtime: goja EventLoop + exec.spawn for non-blocking I/O
codebase_facts.state_machine: IDLE → CONFIG → PLAN_GENERATION → PLAN_REVIEW → PLAN_EDITOR → BRANCH_BUILDING → EQUIV_CHECK → FINALIZATION → DONE
codebase_facts.integration_constraint: Valid integration tests MUST execute the actual osm binary — not just JS unit tests

tasks.title: ARCHITECTURE: Rearchitect prompt/input anchor stability for Submit interactions — element positioning must be provably stable
tasks.description: CRITICAL ERROR REPORTED: 'failed to send prompt to Claude: unable to locate stable prompt/input anchors before submit'. This error occurs when the TUI attempts to route a user interaction (keyboard Enter or mouse click) to a Submit action, but the underlying element's positioning anchor cannot be reliably located at the moment of submission. Root causes analysis required: (1) ZONE TRACKING: The Ask Claude overlay (pr_split_16_tui_core.js initClaudeConvo, updateClaudeConvo, viewClaudeConvo) opens an input field with a Submit button. Is the Submit button zone-marked? The zone must be registered BEFORE the view is rendered and remain valid until the button is clicked. Audit: (a) viewClaudeConvo renders a zone.mark('claude-convo-submit', ...) button — confirm it exists and is consistent across all render passes. (b) On overlay open (initClaudeConvo): when is the zone registered? Is there a race between overlay rendering and zone availability? (c) On overlay close: are zones cleaned up? Does a stale zone registration cause anchor mismatches on the next overlay open? (2) ELEMENT POSITIONING STABILITY: The Submit button's absolute position in the terminal (row, column) must remain constant between render and input handling. The overlay dimensions are computed from s.width and s.height. Are these accurate/current? When the user presses Enter, does the event loop see the SAME s.width/s.height as the view function did? If the terminal was resized between render and input, the coordinates drift and anchors become invalid. Audit: (a) Does BubbleTea call _updateFn on WindowSize BEFORE processing KeyPress events? (b) Is s.width/s.height updated synchronously on window resize? (c) Are overlay dimensions cached or recomputed on every view call? (3) INPUT INTERCEPTION: The input field text submission (Enter key) must be routed correctly. Is Ctrl+Enter implemented for newlines? Does standard Enter always submit? Audit: (a) updateClaudeConvo key handlers: is there a race where normal Enter is intercepted at the wrong level? (b) The zone.mark for the Submit button — is it only active when the overlay is open? Are there phantom zones from previous overlays? (4) VISUAL/RENDERING CONSISTENCY: The overlay must render at a fixed position and size. Does lipgloss rendering cause pixel shifts between render passes? Audit: All lipgloss.NewStyle().width().padding().border() calls in viewClaudeConvo — confirm computed widths are deterministic and bounds-checked against s.width. REQUIRED DELIVERABLES: (1) Write a comprehensive audit document (docs/pr-split-prompt-anchor-stability.md) that investigates all four root causes. Include: (a) Zone lifecycle diagram: when zones are registered, when they're checked during input, when they're cleared. (b) Overlay coordinate mapping: how (row, col) is computed for the overlay and Submit button, and proof that they're stable across a full user interaction cycle. (c) Input routing trace: from raw BubbleTea KeyPress event through updateClaudeConvo to Submit execution. (d) Test case stub: pseudocode for a test that reproduces the error (simulating terminal resize or stale zone state). (2) Identify the SPECIFIC line number and code pattern in pr_split_16_tui_core.js or pr_split_15_tui_views.js where the anchor check fails. The error message itself ('unable to locate stable prompt/input anchors') must be grep-able in the code. (3) Propose a rearchitected design: (a) Option A: Pre-compute and cache overlay anchor positions in s (e.g., s.claudeConvoAnchor = {top, left, width}) on overlay open, then refer to cached anchors during input handling. Invalidate cache on any view parameter change (width, height). (b) Option B: Move all zone registration to a dedicated 'zone registry' module that guarantees consistency — zones are registered once and reused. (c) Option C: Use BubbleTea's built-in mouse region tracking instead of manual bubblezone; let BubbleTea handle anchor stability. (4) Create a unit test fixture: _testClaudeConvoAnchorStability() that (a) renders viewClaudeConvo, (b) extracts zone marks from the output, (c) simulates a terminal resize, (d) re-renders, (e) re-extracts zones, (f) asserts all zones remain at identical coordinates OR move to new but consistent coordinates. (5) ACCEPTANCE: The audit document is comprehensive, all four root causes are investigated (even if some are ruled out), the error's source code is identified, a rearchitected design is proposed, and a test fixture proves the solution prevents the error under simulated resize/input races. No partial acceptance — this is foundational.

tasks.title: Audit startAnalysis and runAnalysisAsync for all 'Processing... forever' hang scenarios
tasks.description: The 'Processing... forever' bug is a POTENTIALLY PREVIOUS correctness failure. Comprehensively audit the full analysis pipeline: (1) startAnalysis() triggers isProcessing=true and returns tea.tick(100, 'analysis-poll'). (2) runAnalysisAsync() is the Promise-based async function; it must set s.isProcessing=false on ALL exit paths (success, error, validation-fail, no-changes). (3) handleAnalysisPoll() must keep re-scheduling the tick while s.analysisRunning=true, AND must stop cleanly when s.isProcessing=false AND s.analysisRunning=false. (4) Find race conditions: if s.isProcessing is cleared before s.analysisRunning, the poll may stop scheduling before the success path runs. (5) Find edge cases: what happens if analyzeDiffAsync hangs indefinitely? There is no timeout, no user-visible progress indicator beyond 'Processing...'. (6) What happens if the git repo has no base branch? configValidationError must be set, the modal must close. (7) Produce a written audit in docs/ of all hang scenarios identified and their status (fixed/open).

tasks.title: Add global analysis timeout with user-visible countdown and abort option
tasks.description: The analysis pipeline has NO timeout protection. If analyzeDiffAsync hangs (e.g., git credential prompt, slow network, large repo), the user sees 'Processing...' forever with no way to abort except Ctrl+C (which doesn't kill the subprocess clearly). Implement: (1) A configurable timeout (default 60s, configurable via prSplitConfig.analysisTimeoutMs). (2) When the timeout fires (via another tea.tick check in handleAnalysisPoll), display a 'Taking longer than expected...' message in the analysis screen. (3) At 2x timeout, surface a 'This is taking unusually long — click to abort' button in the screen. (4) Abort path must kill the running exec.spawn subprocess via the cancellation mechanism. (5) Add a corresponding unit test verifying the timeout message appears at the correct threshold.

tasks.title: Audit the full startVerifySession → pollVerifySession → runVerifyBranch PTY pipeline
tasks.description: The async verify pipeline exists in code but has never been end-to-end validated through an actual binary run. The specific audit must cover: (1) startVerifySession in pr_split_06_verification.js — does it correctly create a git worktree, start the CaptureSession, and return {session, worktreeDir, startTime}? What happens if the worktree dir already exists? (2) pollVerifySession — does it correctly detect isDone(), read exitCode(), capture output(), call cleanupVerifyWorktree(), and push to verificationResults[]? (3) runVerifyBranch — verify skip logic, dependency checking, fallback to verifySplitAsync, and correct state transitions. (4) handleVerifyFallbackPoll — verify it correctly advances after async completion. (5) Write a Go test (or extend pr_split_06_verification_test.go) that runs startVerifySession with a real shell command and validates full lifecycle. (6) Confirm CaptureSession.Interrupt() and Kill() behave correctly from JS via the bound JS method.

tasks.title: Confirm CaptureSession JS binding exposes all required methods (isDone, exitCode, output, close, interrupt, kill)
tasks.description: The JS code in runVerifyBranch and pollVerifySession calls: s.activeVerifySession.isDone(), .exitCode(), .output(), .close(), .interrupt(), .kill(). These must be bound from termmux.CaptureSession to the Goja runtime via internal/builtin/termmux. Read internal/builtin/termmux to confirm: (1) All six methods are exposed. (2) output() returns a string (not bytes). (3) isDone() returns a bool. (4) exitCode() returns an int. (5) interrupt() and kill() both work from JS context. (6) close() properly releases resources. Write a unit test in pr_split_pty_unix_test.go or termmux_observation_test.go that creates a real CaptureSession from JS and validates all methods.

tasks.title: Fix verify live viewport rendering in viewExecutionScreen (Screen 5) to use ANSI rendering
tasks.description: The current verify live viewport in viewExecutionScreen uses s.activeVerifySession.output() which returns raw PTY output (VT100/ANSI escape codes). The current code does a naive truncation with string.substring() which corrupts ANSI codes. Fix: (1) Replace the VT100-aware output source: use s.activeVerifySession.Screen() which returns the rendered VT100 screen (clean text per-line), or use lipgloss.newStyle().maxWidth().render() for ANSI-safe truncation. (2) The verify viewport bordered box must correctly calculate its content width: subtract border(2) + padding(2) from the available width. (3) Verify the scrollbar math: startLine, endLine, totalLines, verifyViewportOffset must work correctly at all height settings. (4) Add a unit test that feeds known ANSI escape sequences into the verify view and confirms they don't corrupt the rendered output.

tasks.title: Implement manual-complete button for verify screen (user can mark a branch as passed)
tasks.description: Design requirement: verification is meant to be interactive. The user may need to install dependencies or set up an environment before the verify command succeeds. They must be able to: mark a branch as manually verified (override the result). Implement: (1) Add a 'Mark as Passed' button in viewExecutionScreen when activeVerifySession IS active (the branch is currently being verified). (2) Zone mark: 'verify-manual-pass'. (3) Handler: in handleMouseClick for BRANCH_BUILDING state, clicking 'verify-manual-pass' should: stop the active session (sendSIGINT then cleanup), record verificationResults[verifyingIdx] = {passed: true, manuallyVerified: true, ...}, advance verifyingIdx, continue with verify-branch tick. (4) Add the element to getFocusElements for BRANCH_BUILDING screen. (5) Handle keyboard activation (Enter on focused element). (6) Write a unit test verifying the manual-pass flow stores the correct result.

tasks.title: Implement 'Open Shell in Worktree' action for verify screen
tasks.description: Design requirement: user should be able to open an interactive shell in the verify worktree to manually set up the environment (npm install, go mod download, etc.). Implement: (1) Add an 'Open Shell' button in viewExecutionScreen when activeVerifySession IS active and s.activeVerifyWorktree is set. (2) Zone mark: 'verify-open-shell'. (3) Handler: on click, spawn an interactive PTY session in s.activeVerifyWorktree. Use tuiMux to attach the new PTY as the Claude pane substitute (or open a dedicated termmux pane). (4) The active verify session is PAUSED (not killed) while the shell is open. (5) After the shell exits, the verify pane resumes polling (the original verify command may still be running or needs restarting). (6) Document this in the UI with a tooltip. (7) If tuiMux.spawnInteractive() API doesn't exist, define the interface and add a TODO for the Go implementation.

tasks.title: Update docs/pr-split-tui-design.md: rewrite verification section for PTY-based interactive design
tasks.description: The current design doc describes verification as a simple 'run command and show result' flow. This must be completely rewritten to reflect: (1) Verify command runs in a PTY (pty.Start), not a plain exec.Command. (2) Live output is streamed to the TUI in a scrollable bordered viewport in Screen 5 (BRANCH_BUILDING). (3) User can interrupt (Ctrl+C / 2×Ctrl+C for force-kill) the verify process. (4) User can manually mark a branch as passed. (5) User can open an interactive shell in the worktree. (6) After verification completes, the next branch starts automatically. (7) Split-view is available during verification (Ctrl+L shows Claude pane alongside verify output). (8) Screen 6 (EQUIV_CHECK) shows the post-verification equivalence results. Produce a complete rewrite of the relevant section with ASCII mocks of the new UI states.

tasks.title: Add binary E2E test: TestBinaryE2E_VerifyPTYLive
tasks.description: Write a binary E2E test that: (1) Builds the osm binary. (2) Creates a test git repo with multiple changed files across directories to generate a 2+ branch split. (3) Runs 'osm pr-split -base=main -strategy=directory -verify=sleep 0.5' (a command that takes ≥0.5s so the TUI can observe it running). (4) Uses PTY to navigate: past CONFIG → trigger execution → observe BRANCH_BUILDING screen showing the verify subprocess running → observe the live output viewport appearing. (5) Confirms the TUI reaches FINALIZATION or at least completes all branches. (6) Confirms 'Verifying Branches' section appears in the BRANCH_BUILDING screen output. (7) Test timeout: 120 seconds.

tasks.title: Add binary E2E test: TestBinaryE2E_CancelDuringVerify
tasks.description: Write a test that: (1) Starts osm pr-split with -verify='sleep 30' (long-running verify). (2) Navigates to BRANCH_BUILDING screen where verify is running. (3) Sends Ctrl+C to interrupt the active verify session. (4) Confirms the TUI shows the interrupted state (result recorded as failed/interrupted). (5) Sends Ctrl+C a second time within 2 seconds (force-kill path). (6) Confirms worktrees are cleaned up (temp dir is removed). (7) Confirms no zombie processes remain after binary exits. Test must complete within 60 seconds.

tasks.title: Fix focusedButton style: secondary buttons must not lose their border when focused
tasks.description: CONFIRMED BUG: styles.focusedButton() applies background(COLORS.warning) WITHOUT a border style. styles.secondaryButton() applies border(roundedBorder()). When a secondaryButton is focused (e.g., 'Test Connection', 'Edit Plan', field values in advanced config), it switches to focusedButton() which removes the border, causing the button to visually collapse/shrink and change shape. Fix: (1) Introduce styles.focusedSecondaryButton() that combines: bold(true), foreground(COLORS.textOnColor), background(COLORS.warning), padding(0,2), border(lipgloss.roundedBorder()), borderForeground(COLORS.warning). This maintains the same visual width as secondaryButton while highlighting focus. (2) Audit ALL locations in pr_split_15_tui_views.js where focusedButton() is applied to elements that normally render as secondaryButton, and replace with focusedSecondaryButton(). Known affected elements: Test Connection button, config field values in advanced section when focused, strategy radio buttons when focused. (3) Add a unit test that measures lipgloss.width() of focused vs unfocused state and asserts they are equal.

tasks.title: Add nav-cancel to all getFocusElements cycles for keyboard accessibility
tasks.description: CONFIRMED BUG: getFocusElements() in pr_split_16_tui_core.js does NOT include 'nav-cancel' for any wizard screen. This means the Cancel button is entirely keyboard-inaccessible for users who navigate by Tab. Fix: (1) Add {id: 'nav-cancel', type: 'nav'} to the focus elements list for ALL states where isProcessing is false. (2) Determine correct placement: typically after the back button and before split-specific elements, OR at the end of the list (Tab to get to Cancel last). Choose consistently. (3) In handleFocusActivate(), add a handler for focused.id === 'nav-cancel' that triggers the cancel confirmation (same as clicking nav-cancel zone). (4) Add the corresponding activation logic (sets s.showConfirmCancel = true OR triggers verify interrupt if active). (5) Add a unit test verifying nav-cancel appears in the focus list for CONFIG, PLAN_REVIEW, PLAN_EDITOR, and FINALIZATION states.

tasks.title: Audit and fix border alignment for all card styles across all screens
tasks.description: Border misalignment is reported as a known bug. Audit: (1) styles.focusedCard() uses doubleBorder() — which adds 1 char on each side. styles.activeCard() and styles.inactiveCard() — what border do they use? The switch from normalBorder to doubleBorder changes the visual width by 0 (doubleBorder uses the same box-drawing chars as normalBorder for top/bottom/left/right). BUT if normalBorder vs roundedBorder are mixed, there are subtle rounding differences in corners. (2) In viewPlanReviewScreen, confirm all card styles use consistent width() settings that account for border width correctly. The card width should be: s.width - 8 - 2 (for border on each side). If .padding(1,2) is used, subtract 4 from content width. (3) In viewPlanEditorScreen, same audit. (4) Ensure no card overflows the terminal width. (5) Write a parameterized unit test at multiple terminal widths (40, 60, 80, 100, 120) verifying lipgloss.width() of rendered cards does not exceed the available width.

tasks.title: Screen 1 (CONFIG): Full keyboard navigation audit and fix
tasks.description: Exhaustive audit of Screen 1 keyboard navigation: (1) Tab order: strategy-auto → strategy-heuristic → strategy-directory → [test-claude if visible] → toggle-advanced → [advanced fields if expanded] → nav-cancel → nav-next. (7) Escape from inline edit mode restores the field. (8) Confirm no phantom focus states where focusIndex leads to an element that isn't rendered (e.g., when showAdvanced is false but focusIndex points to an advanced field). Add unit tests for each Tab-stop transition.

tasks.title: Screen 3 (PLAN_REVIEW): Fix card selection vs focus collision and ensure consistent styling
tasks.description: Audit Screen 3 rendering: (1) A card can be BOTH focused (focusIndex === i) AND selected (selectedSplitIdx === i) at the same time. Current code applies focusedCard style if focused, activeCard if selected. When BOTH are true, focusedCard takes precedence (correct). BUT: if the user tabs PAST the card (to Edit Plan button), the card should revert to activeCard or inactiveCard styling depending on whether it's selected. Verify this works correctly by tracing through viewPlanReviewScreen with various focusIndex/selectedSplitIdx combinations. (2) In wide layout, the left card list uses firstLine rendering without boxed card styles — verify the focus indicator (▸) renders correctly and aligns with the right detail pane. (3) The 'Execute Plan →' button in the nav bar must only activate when there's at least one split in the plan. Write unit tests for all selection/focus combinations.

tasks.title: Screen 4 (PLAN_EDITOR): Fix inline title edit cursor rendering and edit buffer consistency
tasks.description: Audit Screen 4 inline title editing: (1) When editorTitleEditing=true, the edit buffer (editorTitleText) is rendered with a cursor character (█/▏). The cursor must blink, OR be static but clearly visible. Confirm the current implementation shows the cursor correctly at the END of the text. (2) When the user types and then tabs to another element, the edit must be SAVED (or a warning shown). Currently editorTitleEditing may not be auto-committed. Write unit tests for all three.

Confirm updateMoveDialog correctly moves the file and updates selectedFileIdx. Edge case: moving the LAST file from a split — should the split be removed automatically or show a validation error? tasks.title: Global chrome: fix side effects in view functions (auto-dismiss logic must move to update)
tasks.description: BUG CATEGORY: Pure view functions (renderStatusBar, renderClaudePane, etc.) contain state mutation (side effects). This violates the elm-architecture principle and can cause subtle bugs where the view creates phantom state transitions. CONFIRMED instances: (1) renderStatusBar (pr_split_15_tui_views.js ~line 480): sets s.claudeAutoAttachNotif = '' and s.claudeAutoAttachNotifAt = 0 INSIDE the view to auto-dismiss the Claude auto-attach notification. This means the notification is cleared as a side-effect of rendering — if View is called in a test or inspection context the notification will vanish without a user event. Fix: In the _updateFn handler that SETS s.claudeAutoAttachNotif, also dispatch a tea.tick(5000, 'dismiss-attach-notif'). Then add a handler in _updateFn: if (msg.type === 'dismiss-attach-notif') { s.claudeAutoAttachNotif = ''; s.claudeAutoAttachNotifAt = 0; }. Remove all such mutations from renderStatusBar. (2) _viewFn (pr_split_16_tui_core.js ~lines 1005-1035): sets s._isNavNextFocused and calls s.vp.setWidth/setHeight — see T120 for the specific fix. (3) viewReportOverlay (pr_split_15_tui_views.js ~lines 1938-1950): calls s.reportVp.setWidth(), s.reportVp.setHeight(), s.reportSb.setViewportHeight(), s.reportSb.setContentHeight(), s.reportSb.setYOffset() — these five calls mutate the report viewport and scrollbar in every render; see T072 and T080 for specific fixes. Audit ALL render functions for state mutations — search for any assignment to `s.` inside any function passed to viewForState or any function named render*/view*. Required deliverable: (4) Create a tea.tick('notification-dismiss') and handle it in _updateFn to auto-dismiss after 5s. (5) Write a test helper that calls every view function with a frozen (Object.freeze) state copy and asserts no TypeError is thrown — this catches all remaining view mutations.

tasks.title: Performance audit: 'auto' grouping strategy + large repos (100+ changed files)

For TUI + PLAN_GENERATION state, this all runs in runAnalysisAsync which IS async (uses prSplit._gitExecAsync) — but the groupByDependency file reads are STILL sync inside the async function because they use exec.execv, not exec.spawn. Full audit: (1) Create a benchmark test: git repo with 200 changed .go files, measure time for selectStrategy() to complete. (2) Measure how many ms the Goja event loop is blocked. If it exceeds 100ms, the TUI misses frames. (3) Proposed fix (see T092): create groupByDependencyAsync() that uses exec.spawn for file reads, and selectStrategyAsync() that awaits all 5 strategies. (4) Also test 100-file go repo with all other grouping strategies to confirm they're fast (<10ms each). (5) Memory: confirm no unbounded growing of outputLines/verifyOutput arrays (cap at 5000 lines — verify it works). (6) Add benchmarks for plan review rendering at 50+ splits. tasks.title: Enhance viewVerificationScreen: add expandable warning details and tree hash display
tasks.description: The current viewVerificationScreen is minimal (17 lines of logic). Enhance: (1) When equiv.equivalent is false, show the file-level diff: which files differ between the combined split tree and the source tree (if that data is available in equiv). (2) Add a 'Details' expandable section showing the exact tree hash comparison with context. (3) When equiv.skippedBranches is non-empty, show which branches were skipped and WHY (the skippedBranches field should include reason strings, not just names). tasks.title: Fix viewHelpOverlay to be context-aware and include all keybindings
tasks.description: CONFIRMED GAP (second session read): viewHelpOverlay is NOT context-aware — it always shows all sections regardless of current wizard state. tasks.title: Fix viewConfirmCancelOverlay: add keyboard Tab focus cycling within the overlay
tasks.description: CONFIRMED BUG (second session read): viewConfirmCancelOverlay has two buttons ('Yes, Cancel' and 'No, Continue') but neither is in the focus cycle when the overlay is open. The overlay intercepts ALL key events in updateConfirmCancel but does not maintain a focusable element list. Fix: (1) Add a local focus index (overlayFocusIdx) to the cancel confirmation state. (2) Tab/Shift+Tab cycles between the two buttons. (3) 'y' or Enter on focused 'Yes' cancels. (4) 'n' or Enter on focused 'No' dismisses. (7) Extend updateConfirmCancel to handle Tab/Shift+Tab. (8) Write unit tests for both keyboard navigation paths through the overlay.

tasks.title: Clean up stale .pr-split-plan.json artifact in internal/command/

tasks.title: Verify 'Create PRs' TUI button flow: handleFinalizationState → createPRs → skipped PR display
tasks.description: AUDIT COMPLETE (session 4): pr_split_07_prcreation.js confirmed using gh CLI via synchronous exec.execv (T076), ignoring dryRun (T077), and not surfacing skip results to TUI (T083).
