tasks.title: Add binary E2E test: TestBinaryE2E_PlanEditorFlow
tasks.description: Write a binary E2E test that exercises the plan editor (Screen 4): (1) Build osm binary. (2) Create test repo with 4+ changed files in 2+ directories. (3) Navigate: CONFIG (Enter) → wait for plan → PLAN_REVIEW → press 'e' to enter editor. (4) In editor: Tab to select a split card → Tab to 'Move File' button → Enter to open dialog → navigate to select a target split → Enter to confirm move. (5) Tab to 'Rename Split' → Enter → type new name → Enter. (6) Navigate back to PLAN_REVIEW via 'Done'. (7) Verify the plan shows the updated split names and file assignment. (8) Continue to execution (Enter on Execute Plan). (9) Verify the binary exits cleanly with git branches matching the edited plan.

tasks.title: Enhance scopedVerifyCommand to support npm test, pytest, and cargo test scoping
tasks.description: scopedVerifyCommand in pr_split_00_core.js currently only scopes Go test commands (when files are *.go and command is 'make' or contains 'go test'). Extend: (1) Detect TypeScript/JavaScript files (*.ts, *.tsx, *.js, *.jsx) + npm/jest commands → scope to jest --testPathPattern. (2) Detect Python files (*.py) + pytest command → scope to pytest with specific file paths. (3) Detect Rust files (*.rs) + cargo command → scope to cargo test with specific module patterns. (4) The scoping logic should be configurable via prSplitConfig.verifyScope = {go: true, jest: true, pytest: false, cargo: true}. (5) For unrecognized file types, fall back to the full command (no scoping). (6) Write unit tests for each language detection case.

tasks.title: Fix side effects in viewReportOverlay: scrollbar update must move to tick handler
tasks.description: CONFIRMED BUG (sessions 3 and 11): viewReportOverlay in pr_split_15_tui_views.js calls FIVE state-mutating methods on every render: (1) s.reportVp.setWidth(overlayW - 2), (2) s.reportVp.setHeight(overlayH - 6), (3) s.reportSb.setViewportHeight(overlayH - 6), (4) s.reportSb.setContentHeight(s.reportVp.totalLineCount()), (5) s.reportSb.setYOffset(s.reportVp.YOffset). These are all state mutations inside a BubbleTea view function and violate the elm-architecture principle. Concrete impact: (a) Between two BubbleTea render passes (e.g., during resize handling), calling viewReportOverlay mutates reportSb's content height — if called for snapshot testing, the scrollbar model drifts. (b) s.reportSb.setYOffset inside view can silently reset scroll position if the viewport hasn't scrolled yet. Fix: (1) Move dimension updates to _updateFn: when msg.type === 'open-report' fires, initialise s.reportVp.setWidth/setHeight and s.reportSb.setViewportHeight/setContentHeight. (2) On WindowSize: update if s.showingReport is true. (3) On 'report-scroll' (j/k presses), update only s.reportSb.setYOffset after viewport scrolls. (4) viewReportOverlay must become read-only: only call s.reportVp.view() and s.reportSb.view() — zero writes. (5) Write a test: freeze `s` before calling viewReportOverlay, assert no TypeError (no mutations). Note: T080 overlaps with this task (same function, viewport dimension mutations); these two tasks can be implemented as a single commit.

tasks.title: Implement 'c copy' shortcut in Report overlay (currently advertised but not implemented)
tasks.description: CONFIRMED GAP (session 2 + session 4): viewHelpOverlay shows 'c copy' hint in the report overlay, but the copy shortcut is NOT handled anywhere in the update or report overlay handlers. Additionally CONFIRMED (session 4): the REPL 'copy' command in pr_split_14_tui_commands.js calls output.toClipboard(text) — but this method does NOT exist in the scripting API (no Go binding implements it). The REPL copy command will throw at runtime. Implementation requires: (1) PREREQUISITE — implement output.toClipboard() as a real binding in the scripting module (see T088). OS-level fallback: on macOS use 'pbcopy'; on Linux use 'xclip -selection clipboard' or 'wl-copy'; on Windows use 'clip'. (2) In the report overlay key handling (in the update loop where showingReport=true), intercept 'c' → call output.toClipboard(s.reportContent). (3) Show a brief success flash in the status bar: 'Copied to clipboard!' auto-dismissing after 3s. (4) If clipboard is not available (headless/SSH), catch the error and display 'Clipboard not available in this environment'. (5) Write a unit test that fires the 'c' key in report overlay state and verifies output.toClipboard is called with the expected content.

tasks.title: Audit isCancelled/isPaused/isForceCancelled in the automated pipeline for correctness
tasks.description: pr_split_10_pipeline.js's automatedSplit function calls isCancelled() and isForceCancelled() at various points in the loop. Audit: (1) Read the full automated pipeline's cancellation check points. Are they at EVERY async await point? Or only at top-level loop boundaries? If a cancellation happens during a git operation, will it be detected promptly? (2) The cleanup path on cancellation: does automatedSplit clean up the Claude process, worktrees, and temp files? (3) prSplit.isCancelled() is set via prSplit._cancelSource — confirm this is correctly wired in pr_split.go. (4) In the TUI, Ctrl+C sets s.isProcessing=false + transitions to CANCELLED. Does this trigger the _cancelSource callback? (5) Write a test that simulates cancellation mid-pipeline and confirms all resources are cleaned up.

tasks.title: Expand viewVerificationScreen to show per-branch diff details on EQUIV_CHECK failure
tasks.description: CONFIRMED (session 5): verifyEquivalenceDetailed() in pr_split_06_verification.js (lines 266-295) has been read. Its augmented result object contains: { equivalent, splitTree, sourceTree, error, diffFiles: string[], diffSummary: string }. diffFiles = array of file paths from 'git diff --name-only lastSplit sourceBranch'. diffSummary = output of 'git diff --stat lastSplit sourceBranch'. NOTE: there is NO verifyEquivalenceDetailedAsync variant — startEquivCheck in the TUI currently calls verifyEquivalenceAsync (not the Detailed variant). Implement: (1) Create verifyEquivalenceDetailedAsync() in pr_split_06_verification.js that mirrors verifyEquivalenceAsync but also runs 'git diff --stat/--name-only' async. Export it. (2) In startEquivCheck (pr_split_16_tui_core.js), replace the verifyEquivalenceAsync call with verifyEquivalenceDetailedAsync. Store the result (including diffFiles/diffSummary) in s.equivalenceResult. (3) In viewVerificationScreen, when s.equivalenceResult.equivalent===false and s.equivalenceResult.diffFiles has entries, show an expandable 'Files differing from source' section. Display count: 'N files differ'. Expand/collapse with 'e' key or zone click. (4) Each file entry shows the full relative path. (5) Also show s.equivalenceResult.diffSummary in a faint gray below (git diff --stat format). (6) When skippedBranches is non-empty, show: 'Note: N branches were skipped — equivalence expected to fail.' (7) Write unit tests with mock equivalenceResult objects for all states: equivalent=true, equivalent=false with files, equivalent=false with skippedBranches.

tasks.title: createPRs uses sync exec.execv for all GH CLI and git push calls — freezes TUI
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_07_prcreation.js: createPRs() uses synchronous exec.execv for EVERY operation — git push (per branch), gh pr create (per branch), and gh pr merge (if auto-merge enabled). On a 10-branch stack with 500ms network latency each, this blocks the Goja event loop for 5+ seconds, completely freezing the TUI. Implement: (1) Create createPRsAsync() that uses prSplit._gitExecAsync and exec.spawn equivalents for all gh CLI calls. (2) Wire the TUI's 'final-create-prs' button click to an async poll-based flow (like executeSplitAsync) so the TUI remains responsive. (3) Show a real-time progress view in the FINALIZATION screen while PRs are being created — one row per split with status icons. (4) On failure, show which push/PR failed with the gh CLI error message. (5) The synchronous createPRs() must be kept for REPL/CLI usage — only the TUI path needs the async variant. (6) Write tests verifying per-PR progress events reach the TUI state.

tasks.title: createPRs silently ignores runtime.dryRun — creates real PRs in dry-run mode
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_07_prcreation.js: createPRs() does NOT check prSplit.runtime.dryRun anywhere. If a user configures dry-run mode in the wizard config screen, the 'Create PRs' button in finalization will still push branches and create real GitHub PRs. Fix: (1) In createPRs() (and the async wrapper T076 creates), check `prSplit.runtime.dryRun` at the start. (2) In dry-run mode, simulated push and PR creation output should be logged to results with a '[DRY RUN]' prefix. (3) The TUI finalization screen should display 'DRY RUN — no changes pushed' prominently in the PR creation results area. (4) Add a unit test: run createPRs with runtime.dryRun=true and confirm exec.execv is never called with 'push' or 'gh'.

tasks.title: resolveConflicts uses sync exec.execv for verify command — freezes TUI during conflict resolution
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_08_conflict.js: resolveConflicts() contains two synchronous exec.execv calls for running the verify command: (1) The initial verify: `var verifyResult = exec.execv(['sh', '-c', 'cd ' + shellQuote(worktreeDir) + ' && ' + verifyCommand])` — if verifyCommand takes 30s (e.g., full test suite), the TUI freezes for 30s. (2) The re-verify after each strategy fix: `var reVerify = exec.execv(...)`. Both are called inside an async function body but are actually synchronous because exec.execv is a blocking call on the Goja runtime, not Promise-based. Fix: (1) Use prSplit._gitExecAsync or exec.spawn equivalents for verify command execution. (2) Since resolveConflicts is used in the automated pipeline (not directly from TUI), add a progressFn callback parameter so callers can update UI state. (3) The fix strategy functions themselves also use sync exec.execv for go mod tidy, npm install, etc. — audit ALL strategy.fix() functions and async-ify the subprocess calls where they may take >1s. (4) Add tests that verify resolveConflicts does not block the event loop (use a timer-based check).

tasks.title: VALID_TRANSITIONS missing EQUIV_CHECK → PLAN_REVIEW — user cannot go back from equivalence check
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_13_tui.js VALID_TRANSITIONS: EQUIV_CHECK only transitions to FINALIZATION, ERROR_RESOLUTION, CANCELLED, or ERROR. There is no path back to PLAN_REVIEW. This means: if the equivalence check fails (e.g., tree hash mismatch) and the user wants to adjust the plan (re-split differently), they must cancel and start over. Fix: (1) Add 'PLAN_REVIEW' to VALID_TRANSITIONS['EQUIV_CHECK']. (2) Add a 'Revise Plan' button to viewVerificationScreen (EQUIV_CHECK) that triggers transition to PLAN_REVIEW. (3) The nav-back button in the nav bar must also work from EQUIV_CHECK (currently Back is shown for stepIdx > 0, but EQUIV_CHECK is step 5, so Back IS shown — but there's no handler for it in EQUIV_CHECK state). (4) Audit STATE_TO_STEP map — EQUIV_CHECK is step 5, PLAN_REVIEW is step 2. A back from step 5 → step 2 should work. (5) Confirm the nav-back handler in pr_split_16_tui_core.js correctly handles EQUIV_CHECK → PLAN_REVIEW. (6) Write transition tests.

tasks.title: viewReportOverlay mutates viewport and scrollbar dimensions inside view function
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_15_tui_views.js viewReportOverlay() in session 11 (same confirmation as T072 — they point to the same five-call mutation in the same function). For full detail of the five mutating calls (s.reportVp.setWidth, s.reportVp.setHeight, s.reportSb.setViewportHeight, s.reportSb.setContentHeight, s.reportSb.setYOffset), see T072. T072 and T080 SHOULD BE IMPLEMENTED AS A SINGLE COMMIT since they both address the same view function. This task serves as the 'viewport dimension' sub-task of that combined fix, while T072 is the 'scrollbar sync tick' sub-task. Fix (viewport-specific): (1) Move s.reportVp.setWidth(overlayW - 2) and s.reportVp.setHeight(overlayH - 6) to the update handler: when msg.type === 'open-report' fires AND on WindowSize events (if showingReport). (2) The overlayW/overlayH computation (derived from s.width, s.height, and border margins) should be a pure helper: computeOverlayDimensions(s) → {overlayW, overlayH, contentW, contentH}. This helper is pure (no mutations) and can be called from both _updateFn and viewReportOverlay for layout purposes. (3) viewReportOverlay reads s.reportVp.view() only — no setWidth/setHeight. (4) On terminal resize during open report: _updateFn WindowSize handler calls computeOverlayDimensions + updates reportVp + reportSb. (5) Write test: confirm viewReportOverlay output is deterministic across two calls with frozen state.

tasks.title: viewConfirmCancelOverlay 'All progress will be lost' is misleading — branches NOT cleaned up on cancel
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading viewConfirmCancelOverlay and WizardState.cancel(): the cancel overlay shows 'All progress will be lost' but in BRANCH_BUILDING state, any branches already created via executeSplitAsync REMAIN in the git repo. The branches are NOT cleaned up on cancel. This is misleading and may leave orphaned split branches. Fix: (1) Change the cancel message to be context-aware: if wizardState is BRANCH_BUILDING or later and executionResults.length > 0, show 'N branches created — these will remain in your repo. Clean them up with: osm pr-split --cleanup'. (2) Implement 'clean up and cancel' option that deletes the created branches before cancelling. (3) In WizardState.cancel(), check if cleanupBranches() should be called. (4) The overlay should offer three options: 'Cancel (keep branches)', 'Cancel (delete branches)', 'Continue'. (5) Update the git operations section of docs/configuration.md to document cleanup behavior.

tasks.title: resolveConflicts wall-clock timeout fires silently with no TUI state update
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_08_conflict.js: resolveConflicts() has a wall-clock timeout (AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS.resolveWallClockTimeoutMs || 300000). When it fires, the error pushed to errorsOut is 'wall-clock timeout after Xms (limit: Yms)'. This is never surfaced to the TUI as a user-visible notification — the TUI's ERROR_RESOLUTION screen would just show the raw error string. Fix: (1) Define a structured error type for wall-clock timeout: {type: 'wall-clock-timeout', elapsed, limit, branch}. (2) In the TUI's ERROR_RESOLUTION view, detect this type and show a friendly message: 'Auto-fix timed out for [branch] after [elapsed]s — manual fix required'. (3) Add a countdown display in the TUI during active auto-resolve so users see time remaining. (4) The countdown should use the same tick system as other async ops. (5) Write tests confirming the timeout error type is set correctly and the TUI message matches.

tasks.title: createPRs no-diff skip returns skipped=true but TUI never shows which PRs were skipped
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_07_prcreation.js lines 120-130: when `git diff --quiet base head` returns 0 (no changes), the PR creation sets results[pi].skipped=true and results[pi].skipReason='no changes between X and Y'. However, the TUI finalization screen (viewFinalizationScreen) only shows a list of split names and the equivalence result — it never reads results[pi].skipped. A user could click 'Create PRs', see it apparently complete, and not realize 2 of 5 PRs were silently skipped. Fix: (1) After PR creation completes, store the full results array in s.prCreationResults = results. (2) In viewFinalizationScreen, after PR creation, show per-PR status rows with icons: ✓ created (with URL), — skipped (with reason), ✗ failed (with error). (3) A 'skipped' entry must show the skipReason prominently. (4) Total counts: 'X PRs created, Y skipped, Z failed'. (5) Write tests verifying skipReason appears in the finalization view.

tasks.title: VALID_TRANSITIONS['PAUSED'] has no resume paths — pause feature is a dead-end
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_13_tui.js VALID_TRANSITIONS: PAUSED state can only transition to DONE. PAUSABLE_STATES includes PLAN_GENERATION and BRANCH_BUILDING. But there is NO path from PAUSED back to either of these states. This means once a user pauses (T059 feature), they can only quit — not resume. Fix: (1) Add 'PLAN_GENERATION' and 'BRANCH_BUILDING' to VALID_TRANSITIONS['PAUSED']. (2) Add a 'resume' choice to the pause handling (a 'Resume' button in a paused state overlay). (3) In the TUI, when the wizard is PAUSED (from PLAN_GENERATION context), resuming should transition back to PLAN_GENERATION and re-trigger the analysis poll. From BRANCH_BUILDING context, resuming transitions back to BRANCH_BUILDING and re-triggers the verify poll. (4) The pause context (which state was paused from) must be stored in wizard.data.pausedFrom. (5) Write state machine tests verifying PAUSED → PLAN_GENERATION and PAUSED → BRANCH_BUILDING transitions work. (6) This task is a prerequisite for T059 (pause/resume verify).

tasks.title: saveCheckpoint() does not persist _state caches — resume loses computed data
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_13_tui.js WizardState.saveCheckpoint() and pr_split_03_planning.js savePlan(). WizardState.saveCheckpoint() only saves {current, data} — it does NOT call prSplit.savePlan(), which is the function that persists analysisCache, groupsCache, planCache, executionResultCache, AND conversationHistory to disk. GOOD NEWS: savePlan() (pr_split_03_planning.js lines 115-166) already persists ALL the necessary data to disk in JSON format. loadPlan() already restores them. The fix is SIMPLER than originally described: (1) In WizardState.saveCheckpoint(), add a call to prSplit.savePlan(path, wizard.current) immediately before (or after) the checkpoint object is written. This uses the same DEFAULT_PLAN_PATH as the plan file. (2) The savePlan() call persists analysisCache, groupsCache, planCache, executionResultCache in full. (3) On resume (loadPlan() call in handleConfigState or the 'load-plan' auto-detect on startup), all caches are restored correctly — loadPlan() already performs this restore. (4) Add 'checkpointed: true' flag to the snapshot so the TUI can show 'Resuming from checkpoint' on startup. (5) The REPL 'auto-split --resume' handler's 'not yet implemented' note should be updated to use this mechanism. (6) Write an integration test: checkpoint at BRANCH_BUILDING via saveCheckpoint(), create a new prSplit runtime, call loadPlan(), verify analysisCache and planCache are restored with all splits.

tasks.title: 'edit-plan' REPL command is a stub — implement or document the gap
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_14_tui_commands.js: the 'edit-plan' REPL command handler immediately prints 'Plan editor not available. Use text commands: move, rename, merge.' This is a non-functional stub. Options: (1) Option A: Implement a minimal non-BubbleTea text-driven plan editor in the REPL that prompts for move/rename/merge operations sequentially. (2) Option B: ACCEPTED GAP — officially document that interactive editing is only available in the BubbleTea TUI (osm pr-split -interactive). Update the help text to say: 'Interactive plan editing is available in TUI mode (default). In REPL mode, use: move, rename, merge, reorder commands.' (3) If Option B, mark the help text for the 'edit-plan' command with a clear direction to TUI mode. (4) Either way, remove the misleading suggestion that 'edit-plan' will work and document the REPL text-editing alternative. (5) Update docs/scripting.md to document the REPL editing pattern.

tasks.title: conversationHistory array in prSplit._state is unbounded — cap at N entries to prevent memory leak
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_11_utilities.js: recordConversation() pushes entries to prSplit._state.conversationHistory with no cap. For a long-running auto-split session with many Claude iterations, this could accumulate hundreds to thousands of conversation entries, each containing full prompt and response text. Fix: (1) In recordConversation(), after pushing the new entry, trim the array to a maximum of MAX_CONVERSATION_HISTORY entries (default: 100). (2) Make the cap configurable via prSplitConfig.maxConversationHistory. (3) If trimming is applied, log a warning once per session: 'Conversation history capped at 100 entries — oldest entries discarded.' (4) Also assert a cap on prSplit._state.analysisCache.files — this array could be large for repos with thousands of changed files. The TUI renders all files; ensure renders are paginated or capped. (5) Write a unit test: insert 200 conversations → verify length stays at 100 after each insert.

tasks.title: Implement output.toClipboard() Go binding in scripting module (unblocks T073 + REPL copy)
tasks.description: CONFIRMED (session 4): output.toClipboard(text) is called by the REPL 'copy' command in pr_split_14_tui_commands.js, but this method does NOT exist in the Go scripting module. Any invocation of the 'copy' REPL command currently throws a JavaScript ReferenceError at runtime. Implementation required: (1) In the scripting module's output binding (locate the Go file that implements the 'output' object — likely internal/scripting/output.go or internal/builtin/output/), add a toClipboard(text string) function. (2) Platform-specific implementation: macOS — exec 'pbcopy'; Linux — try 'xclip -selection clipboard' then 'wl-copy' as fallback; Windows — exec 'clip'. (3) If no clipboard utility is available or the exec fails, return a descriptive error ('clipboard not available: pbcopy not found'). (4) Write unit tests for the function: (a) mock exec to return success, confirm content matches. (b) mock exec to fail, confirm error message is returned. (5) This is a prerequisite for T073 (TUI 'c copy' shortcut). After implementation, the REPL 'copy' command will work automatically (no changes needed to pr_split_14_tui_commands.js). (6) Add output.toClipboard documentation to docs/scripting.md.

tasks.title: buildReport() calls verifyEquivalence() synchronously — blocks event loop on finalization
tasks.description: CONFIRMED (session 8): pr_split_13_tui.js buildReport() at line 244 calls prSplit.verifyEquivalence(st.planCache) SYNCHRONOUSLY. FULL CALL PATH CONFIRMED: User clicks 'View Report' → handleFocusActivate(s) in pr_split_16_tui_core.js line 1840 calls formatReportForDisplay(buildReport()) — both in the _updateFn update loop. buildReport() at line 244 of pr_split_13_tui.js then calls prSplit.verifyEquivalence(st.planCache) sync. SECOND SYNC CALL: handleEquivCheckState() at line 685 of pr_split_13_tui.js also calls verifyEquivalence() sync — used by the REPL 'equiv' command, not the TUI path. THE FIX IS CLEAR: (1) In runEquivCheckAsync() (pr_split_16_tui_core.js line ~3270), after storing result in s.equivalenceResult, ALSO store it in st (the shared state): add 'st.equivalenceResult = equivResult'. (2) In buildReport(), replace 'var equiv = prSplit.verifyEquivalence(st.planCache)' with: 'var equiv = st.equivalenceResult || prSplit.verifyEquivalence(st.planCache)'. This short-circuits if already computed (typical TUI path). (3) For handleEquivCheckState() REPL handler at line 685: replace sync call with await verifyEquivalenceAsync() — make handleEquivCheckState async. (4) Write a test: call buildReport() with a pre-populated st.equivalenceResult; confirm verifyEquivalence() is NOT called a second time.

tasks.title: handleConfigState runs synchronous verifySplit() for baseline check — blocks TUI on Enter press
tasks.description: CONFIRMED (session 8): FULL CALL PATH CONFIRMED. pr_split_16_tui_core.js startAnalysis(s) at line 2429 calls handleConfigState({...}) synchronously, INSIDE the _updateFn BubbleTea update loop. handleConfigState (pr_split_13_tui.js line ~365) calls prSplit.verifySplit(runtime.baseBranch, {...}) synchronously for baseline verification. verifySplit() uses exec.execv — BLOCKING. Consequence: when user presses Enter to start analysis, the ENTIRE TUI event loop freezes for the full duration of the baseline verify command. If verifyCommand is 'make test' (30+ seconds), the terminal is completely unresponsive. GOOD NEWS: prSplit.verifySplitAsync() ALREADY EXISTS (confirmed exported from pr_split_06_verification.js). Fix: (1) Make handleConfigState an async function (add 'async' prefix). (2) Replace 'var baselineResult = verifySplit(...)' with 'var baselineResult = await prSplit.verifySplitAsync(...)'. (3) In startAnalysis(s) (pr_split_16_tui_core.js), change the handleConfigState call to be Promise-based: launch as a Promise that feeds into the existing analysisRunning pattern. OR: move the baseline check INTO runAnalysisAsync as a new async step (Step -1: Baseline verify) BEFORE the analyzeDiff step. This keeps the tea.tick polling architecture intact and allows cancellation. (4) Show a new analysis step in the PLAN_GENERATION screen: '✓ Verify baseline [elapsed]ms'. (5) Write a test verifying the TUI does not block for more than 50ms when the baseline verify takes 200ms (mock/async).

tasks.title: Bell handler in pr_split_14 always restores status to 'idle' — previous status lost
tasks.description: CONFIRMED (session 5): the bell handler in pr_split_14_tui_commands.js (line 1268) reads `tuiState._bellPrevStatus || 'idle'` as the status to restore after the 3-second attention flash. BUT `tuiState._bellPrevStatus` is NEVER set anywhere in the codebase (confirmed via grep). So the bell handler always restores status to 'idle', even if the user had set a custom HUD status (e.g., via `tuiMux.setStatus(_renderHudStatusLine())`). The user's custom HUD status is permanently lost whenever a bell fires. Fix: (1) Add `tuiMux.getStatus()` binding to the Go termmux module — returns the current status string that was last set via setStatus(). (2) In the bell handler, before calling tuiMux.setStatus('BELL — Claude needs attention'), capture the current status: `var prevStatus = (typeof tuiMux.getStatus === 'function') ? tuiMux.getStatus() : 'idle'`. (3) Use prevStatus (not tuiState._bellPrevStatus) in the setTimeout restore callback. (4) ALTERNATIVE if (1) is too costly: use a module-level variable in chunk 14 to track `_lastStatusSet` — update it every time setStatus() is called from anywhere in chunk 14. (5) Write a unit test simulating a bell event and verifying the restored status matches what was set before the bell.

tasks.title: Async grouping: 'auto' strategy computes all 5 groupings synchronously — blocks event loop
tasks.description: CONFIRMED (session 5): pr_split_02_grouping.js selectStrategy() runs ALL 5 grouping strategies synchronously, including groupByDependency() which calls osmod.readFile() or exec.execv(['cat', file]) for every .go file to parse imports. For a Go repo with 200 changed .go files, this means 200+ synchronous file I/O calls in a loop. This is called from runAnalysisAsync (async function in pr_split_16_tui_core.js) — but the sync file reads inside groupByDependency block the Goja event loop for the duration. Fix: (1) Create groupByDependencyAsync(files, options) that uses prSplit._gitExecAsync (exec.spawn pattern) for file reads instead of exec.execv. (2) Create selectStrategyAsync(files, options) that awaits each strategy's result. (3) In runAnalysisAsync, replace groupByDirectory/applyStrategy/selectStrategy calls with the async variants. (4) The sync variants (groupByDirectory etc.) must remain for REPL/test use. (5) Note: groupByDirectory, groupByExtension, groupByChunks, groupByPattern are already in-memory (no I/O) — they are fast and need no async variant. Only groupByDependency needs async. (6) Export groupByDependencyAsync and selectStrategyAsync from pr_split_02_grouping.js. (7) Write a benchmark test: 200 go files, confirm selectStrategyAsync completes without blocking the event loop for >50ms.

tasks.title: waitForLogged synchronous fallback blocks Goja event loop for up to 5+ minutes when waitForAsync unavailable
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_10_pipeline.js waitForLogged() in session 6: when mcpCb.waitForAsync is not available on the MCP callback object, the code falls back to synchronous mcpCb.waitFor(toolName, timeoutMs, wrappedOpts). This is a fully blocking call that parks the entire Goja event loop — and therefore BubbleTea — until the MCP tool returns or timeoutMs expires. With classifyTimeoutMs=300000 (5 minutes) and planTimeoutMs=300000, this could block for 10 consecutive minutes with a completely frozen TUI, no spinner, and no cancel option. Fix: (1) Remove the synchronous fallback entirely. If mcpCb.waitForAsync is absent, immediately return a structured failure: {ok: false, error: 'MCP callback missing waitForAsync — cannot proceed with AI classification'}. (2) The Go binding that injects the mcpCallback object MUST always provide waitForAsync; confirm and enforce this in the injection code. (3) If a synchronous fallback is strictly required for backward compatibility in non-TUI REPL mode, guard it with an explicit opt-in flag AND cap its internal timeout at a hard maximum of 10 seconds. (4) Write a unit test verifying waitForLogged returns a structured error when both waitForAsync and waitFor are absent from the mock mcpCb object.

tasks.title: verifyEquivalenceDetailed.diffFiles compares only lastSplit vs source — correctness depends on cumulative branch assumption
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_06_verification.js verifyEquivalenceDetailed() in session 6: builds diffFiles by calling gitExec(dir, ['diff', '--name-only', lastSplit, plan.sourceBranch]) where lastSplit = plan.splits[plan.splits.length - 1].name — always the FINAL split. verifyEquivalence() compares rev-parse lastSplit^{tree} vs sourceBranch^{tree} tree hashes. This is ONLY correct if splits form a CUMULATIVE chain (e.g., split2 is based on split1, progressively accumulating all files — each split includes all prior content plus new changes). If instead splits are INDEPENDENT branches off the same base (each holding only its own files), then lastSplit only has its subset and both the tree hash comparison and the diffFiles output would be wrong/meaningless. REQUIRED INVESTIGATION: (1) Read pr_split_05_execution.js fully to determine whether branches are cumulative or independent. (2) If cumulative chain: add a DESIGN NOTE comment in verifyEquivalence() stating the chain assumption explicitly. (3) If independent: the equivalence check must be redesigned — likely using a combined merge or tree-walk approach. (4) Either way, write an integration test with a known 3-split structure and verify equivalence detection is accurate. (5) Fix verifyEquivalenceDetailed to compare the FULL combined tree vs source if independent.

tasks.title: CRITICAL: TUI 'Create PRs' button is a dead stub — prsRequested flag is never consumed anywhere in the update loop
tasks.description: CRITICAL BUG confirmed by reading pr_split_16_tui_core.js and pr_split_13_tui.js in session 6. The full 'Create PRs' click path: handleFinalizationState(wizard, 'create-prs') is called, which executes ONLY wizard.transition('FINALIZATION', {prsRequested: true}) — a self-transition. Searching pr_split_16_tui_core.js for any handler that reads prsRequested yields ZERO results. The flag is set and never consumed. The REPL 'create-prs' command at line ~411 of pr_split_14_tui_commands.js correctly calls prSplit.createPRs() directly, but the TUI wizard path is a complete dead-end. NOTE: T069 covers the testing work once this is fixed; T076 covers making createPRs() async. This task is the prerequisite: wire up the missing execution bridge in the TUI. Fix: (1) In the FINALIZATION state section of pr_split_16_tui_core.js _updateFn, add a guard check: if wizard.current==='FINALIZATION' && wizard.data.prsRequested===true && !s.prCreationStarted, set s.prCreationStarted=true, s.prCreationInProgress=true and call startCreatePRsAsync(s). (2) startCreatePRsAsync(s) wraps the call to prSplit.createPRs() with Promise.resolve().then(...) to yield the event loop. (3) Wire a 'create-prs-poll' tea.tick for progress updates that writes results to s.prCreationResults[]. (4) Show in-progress state in viewFinalizationScreen while PRs are being created. (5) Once T076 is complete, replace the sync createPRs() call with the async variant. (6) Write a test asserting createPRs is called when the 'Create PRs' zone is clicked.

tasks.title: validatePlan missing branch name character validation — invalid-char split names pass undetected until git fails
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_04_validation.js in session 6 and pr_split_16_tui_core.js updateRenameDialog() in session 10: validateSplitPlan() correctly validates branch name characters using var invalidBranchChars = /[\s~\^:\\\*\?\[]/. But validatePlan() has no such check — it validates only: non-empty splits array, non-empty name, non-empty files, and no duplicate files. A plan with split.name = 'my branch:broken' passes validatePlan() but fails at git branch creation in executeSplit() with a cryptic git error. Highest risk: user-edited plans loaded via --resume from JSON — only validatePlan() is called, not validateSplitPlan(). ADDITIONAL BUG CONFIRMED (session 10): updateRenameDialog() in pr_split_16_tui_core.js (line ~1380) immediately writes the typed new name to st.planCache.splits[splitIdx].name = text on Enter press with NO branch name character validation — an invalid rename passes silently. Fix: (1) In validatePlan(), after checking split.name is non-empty, add: if (invalidBranchChars.test(split.name)) errors.push('Split "' + split.name + '" contains invalid branch name character at position ' + invalidBranchChars.exec(split.name).index + '. Valid names must not contain spaces, tildes, colons, carets, backslashes, asterisks, question marks, or brackets.'). (2) Also validate split.name.trim() is non-empty (handles whitespace-only names). (3) Add the regex check inline or refactor to share the test with validateSplitPlan. (4) In updateRenameDialog() (pr_split_16_tui_core.js), BEFORE writing the new name: check invalidBranchChars.test(text) — if invalid, SET s.editorValidationErrors = ["Invalid branch name: cannot contain [" + char + "]"]; do NOT write to planCache.splits; keep the dialog open. (5) Write unit tests: space in name, colon, tilde, backslash, dot-lock suffix; for rename dialog test, assert editorValidationErrors is set and planCache is unchanged on invalid rename.

tasks.title: validateResolution accepts {preExistingFailure:true} with no patches or commands — silent no-op masks real failures
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_04_validation.js validateResolution() in session 6: a resolution object with ONLY {preExistingFailure: true} — no patches, no commands, no reason — passes validation. If Claude mistakenly returns this for a failure actually caused by the split changes, the resolution is silently accepted, the branch is marked resolved without any fix applied, and it may reach FINALIZATION with hidden failures. Fix: (1) Add validation: if preExistingFailure===true, require a non-empty reason string explaining why — error: 'preExistingFailure:true requires a non-empty reason field'. (2) Add an optional cross-validation mode (prSplitConfig.verifyPreExisting=true): re-run verify on the BASE branch to confirm the failure genuinely exists before this split was applied. (3) Log a prominent WARNING when preExistingFailure=true is accepted: 'WARNING: branch [X] marked as pre-existing failure — verify this is intentional.' (4) Write unit tests: {preExistingFailure: true, reason: 'flaky test that existed before this PR'} → valid. {preExistingFailure: true} with no reason → invalid.

tasks.title: Unknown git status codes in analyzeDiff silently dropped — callers receive fewer files than changed with no notification
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_01_analysis.js analyzeDiff() in session 8 (full read). When a git diff --name-status line has a status code not in KNOWN_STATUSES (A/M/D/R/C/T), the code executes log.warn() and continue — the file is silently dropped from the returned files array with no indication to the caller. CORRECTION from earlier description: 'U' (unmerged) is NOT silently dropped — it already has special handling: it returns immediately with createEmptyResult('unmerged path detected: ...'). But unknown codes like 'X', 'B', 'L', '!' (gitignore-listed) etc. ARE silently dropped with only log.warn. Fix: (1) Add skippedFiles: [{path, status}] to the analyzeDiff() and analyzeDiffAsync() return value and populate it for each call to the unknown-status log.warn path. (2) In the TUI PLAN_GENERATION progress display (handleAnalysisPoll), if result.skippedFiles.length > 0, show a warning card: 'N file(s) with unsupported git status excluded from analysis: [list]'. (3) Also update the KNOWN_STATUSES constant comment to enumerate all handled codes. (4) Write unit tests for 'X', 'B', '!' input status codes confirming skippedFiles is populated; write a separate test for 'U' status confirming the error path already fires (not a new fix needed for U).

tasks.title: selectStrategy.needsConfirm confidence flag never consumed by TUI — ambiguous auto-selection invisible to user
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_02_grouping.js selectStrategy() in session 6: returns needsConfirm: true when the winning strategy's score is within 0.15 of the second-best (low confidence). Also returns scored: [{name, score}] for all candidates. NEITHER field is ever read anywhere in pr_split_16_tui_core.js or any other caller. When auto-selection is uncertain (e.g., directory=0.62 vs dependency=0.58, gap=0.04 < 0.15), users are never informed and the auto choice is presented as authoritative. Fix: (1) In handleAnalysisPoll, after strategy selection completes, check result.needsConfirm. (2) If true: set s.strategyNeedsConfirm=true, s.strategyAlternatives=result.scored. (3) In viewPlanReviewScreen, when s.strategyNeedsConfirm===true, show an amber banner: 'Low confidence: auto-selected [winner] ([pct]%) — [runner-up] ([pct]%) scored similarly. Consider switching strategy.' (4) Add a 'Change Strategy' link/button in the banner navigating back to CONFIG. (5) Write unit tests verifying the banner appears when needsConfirm=true.

tasks.title: analyzeDiffStats silently produces zero additions/deletions for binary files — parseInt('-') = NaN coerced to 0
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_01_analysis.js analyzeDiffStats() in session 6: `git diff --numstat` outputs `- - path` for binary files. The parser executes parseInt(parts[0], 10) || 0 — parseInt('-', 10) returns NaN, and NaN || 0 = 0. Binary files silently appear as having 0 additions and 0 deletions. This causes incorrect file sizing in groupByChunks and directory-deep strategies which weight splits by line counts — binary files are treated as empty (zero-weight) and may all be clumped into a single split regardless of their actual size, creating an unbalanced plan. Fix: (1) Detect binary entries: if (parts[0] === '-' && parts[1] === '-') mark entry as binary: true, additions: null, deletions: null. (2) Optionally estimate size via `git cat-file -s HEAD:path` (blob size in bytes). (3) In size-weighted strategies, assign binary files a configurable fixed weight (default: 50 equivalent lines) so they are distributed rather than concentrated. (4) Write unit tests with mock --numstat output containing binary file lines and assert binary: true is set and additions/deletions are null (not 0).

tasks.title: loadPlan duplicates conversation history if called twice — appends without resetting prior state
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_03_planning.js loadPlan() in session 6: the conversation restore loop is: for (var c = 0; c < snapshot.conversations.length; c++) { state.conversationHistory.push(snapshot.conversations[c]); }. This APPENDS to any existing conversations without clearing first. If loadPlan is called twice on the same session object (e.g., resume → retry → manual reload), all conversations are pushed again — doubling or tripling the history. The Ask Claude overlay then shows duplicated messages. Fix: (1) Replace the push loop with: state.conversationHistory = snapshot.conversations.slice(); — a single-line, idempotent replacement. (2) Add a savedAt timestamp to snapshots in savePlan(): snapshot.savedAt = Date.now(). (3) Add a dedup guard in loadPlan(): if (snapshot.savedAt && snapshot.savedAt === state._lastLoadedSavedAt) return; state._lastLoadedSavedAt = snapshot.savedAt. (4) Write a unit test: call loadPlan twice on the same snapshot; verify state.conversationHistory.length === snapshot.conversations.length (not doubled).

tasks.title: applyStrategy falls back to groupByDirectory for any unknown strategy name with no warning or signal to caller
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_02_grouping.js applyStrategy() in session 6: the switch statement's default: case returns groupByDirectory(files, 1) with NO log.warn, NO thrown error, and NO indicator in the return value that the strategy name was unrecognized. If a user runs `osm pr-split --strategy=my-typo` or if a saved config file references an old strategy name, the tool silently uses directory grouping and the user has no idea why their requested strategy was ignored. Fix: (1) Add to default case: log.warn('pr-split: unknown strategy "' + strategy + '" — falling back to directory. Valid: auto, heuristic, directory, directory-deep, extension, chunks, dependency'). (2) Annotate the return: var result = groupByDirectory(files, 1); result._fallbackUsed = true; result._requestedStrategy = strategy; return result. (3) In the Go CLI (pr_split.go), add strategy to an allowlist validation in Execute() to reject unknown values before reaching JS. (4) In the TUI CONFIG screen handler, if applyStrategy returns _fallbackUsed===true, surface a warning badge. (5) Write unit tests: call applyStrategy with 'invalid-strategy' and confirm log.warn fires and _fallbackUsed===true; call with 'directory' and confirm _fallbackUsed is absent/false.

tasks.title: Worktree path uses 'dir + /../' pattern — fragile at filesystem root or non-writable parent directories
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_05_execution.js executeSplitAsync() in session 6 and pr_split_06_verification.js startVerifySession(): both create temporary git worktrees using the path `dir + '/../.osm-worktree-' + Date.now()`. The `/../` pattern navigates to the PARENT directory of the git repo. This fails (or produces a path collision) in three cases: (1) If dir is at the filesystem root (e.g., dir='/') then '//../.osm-worktree-' is invalid. (2) If the parent directory of the repo is not writable by the current user (e.g., /home/user/myrepo where /home/user is root-owned). (3) Multiple concurrent pr-split invocations targeting the same repo at the same timestamp — the worktree name includes Date.now() but two calls within 1ms would collide. Fix: (1) Use `os.tmpdir() + '/.osm-worktree-' + Date.now() + '-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2,6)` for the worktree path — using the system temp directory avoids write-permission issues. (2) Add more entropy to the name (Math.random suffix) to reduce collision probability. (3) Alternatively: use `dir + '/.osm-worktree-' + ...` (WITHIN the repo) and add `.osm-worktree-*` to the repo's .gitignore. (4) Add worktree path logging at debug level. (5) Write a unit test verifying worktree creation succeeds even when the parent of 'dir' is not writable (mock the gitExecAsync calls).

tasks.title: automatedSplit re-verify step after re-split always returns error:null — failures silently continue to equivalence check
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_10_pipeline.js automatedSplit() in session 6 (lines 1916-1929): after re-classification and re-execution (the re-split fallback when conflicts cannot be resolved), the re-verify step is: `await step('Re-verify splits', async function() { return { error: null, results: await verifySplits(plan) }; })`. This ALWAYS returns `{ error: null }` regardless of whether the re-verified branches pass or fail — the `results` from verifySplits is captured but the step() wrapper only inspects `.error`. If re-split branches still fail verification, the pipeline continues silently to step 10 (equivalence check) with unverified branches. Fix: (1) Change the re-verify step to check the results: `var rv = await verifySplits(plan); var reFails = rv.results.filter(function(r) { return !r.passed && !r.skipped && !r.preExisting; }); if (reFails.length > 0) { return { error: reFails.length + ' branch(es) still fail after re-split: ' + reFails.map(function(r) { return r.name; }).join(', '), failures: reFails }; } return { error: null }`. (2) If re-verify fails, add it to report.conflicts and report.error. (3) Optionally: attempt a second round of conflict resolution on the re-split failures (with a separate pass counter to prevent infinite loops). (4) Write a unit test simulating a re-split where the re-verified branches still fail, and assert the pipeline returns an error.

tasks.title: DEFAULT_PLAN_PATH hardcoded to '.pr-split-plan.json' ignores config.dir — plan file goes to wrong directory
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_03_planning.js and pr_split_10_pipeline.js in sessions 5-6: DEFAULT_PLAN_PATH = '.pr-split-plan.json' is a hardcoded constant that always expands to the CURRENT WORKING DIRECTORY at the time of the call. If the user runs `osm pr-split --dir=/path/to/repo` from a different directory (e.g., /home/user), the plan file is saved to /home/user/.pr-split-plan.json instead of /path/to/repo/.pr-split-plan.json. On resume, the automatic detection also looks in the wrong place. Fix: (1) Change DEFAULT_PLAN_PATH to be a function: `function getDefaultPlanPath(dir) { return (dir || '.') + '/.pr-split-plan.json'; }` (2) Update all call sites of savePlan(null, ...) and loadPlan() to use getDefaultPlanPath(runtime.dir || config.dir || '.'). (3) In automatedSplit(), replace `var DEFAULT_PLAN_PATH = prSplit.DEFAULT_PLAN_PATH` with `var DEFAULT_PLAN_PATH = prSplit.getDefaultPlanPath(dir)`. (4) Add .pr-split-plan.json to the .gitignore of this repository. (5) Write a unit test: configure config.dir = '/tmp/test-repo'; call savePlan; verify the file is created at '/tmp/test-repo/.pr-split-plan.json' (not in CWD).

tasks.title: state.claudeCrashDetected never set in headless (non-TUI) mode — crashed Claude processes undetected by aliveCheckFn
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_10_pipeline.js automatedSplit() in session 6: aliveCheckFn checks `if (state.claudeCrashDetected) { return false; }` before checking `claudeExecutor.handle.isAlive()`. The `state.claudeCrashDetected` flag is intended to be set by the TUI's health monitoring poll (which periodically checks if the Claude process is alive and sets this flag). BUT in headless/non-TUI mode (e.g., automated CI usage of `osm pr-split --auto`), there is no TUI health monitor running — the flag is never set. This means a Claude crash in headless mode may still return isAlive()===false (since the handle's process has exited) — so the isAlive() check DOES still work for detecting crashes. The claudeCrashDetected check is redundant in headless mode. HOWEVER: the flag may be set by the TUI before aliveCheckFn is called, causing it to short-circuit before checking isAlive() — this could mask a temporarily-dead but recovered handle. Fix: (1) Audit how claudeCrashDetected is set/cleared in the TUI health monitor. (2) In headless mode (no tuiMux), skip the claudeCrashDetected check and rely exclusively on handle.isAlive(). (3) Add a guard: `if (state.claudeCrashDetected && hasTUI) { return false; }` to only trust the flag when the TUI is active. (4) Write a test confirming aliveCheckFn returns false when isAlive() returns false even without claudeCrashDetected being set.

tasks.title: executeSplitAsync skips .gitignore-matched files with log.warn only — TUI and report never show which files were omitted
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_05_execution.js executeSplitAsync() in session 6 (lines 254-267): before executing the split, the function checks `git check-ignore --no-index` for all planned files. Any git-ignored files are recorded in `ignoredFiles{}` and skipped during checkout/staging, with only a `log.warn` call noting the skip. The TUI execution screen (BRANCH_BUILDING) never shows which files were skipped, and the final report does not include a 'skipped due to .gitignore' section. Users could create a plan that includes .gitignore-matched files (e.g., generated files, build artifacts that are tracked but listed in a newer .gitignore) and never realize those files are silently excluded. Fix: (1) In the result of executeSplitAsync, include `overallSkippedFiles: [...list of git-ignored files...}` at the top level (not just per-split) so callers can surface this information. (2) In viewExecutionScreen (pr_split_15_tui_views.js), if any splitResult.skippedFiles.length > 0, show a styled warning for that branch: 'Note: N file(s) matched .gitignore and were excluded: [list]'. (3) In buildReport(), include the skippedFiles in the report section. (4) Write a unit test confirming that when git check-ignore returns ignored files, they appear in splitResult.skippedFiles and the overall skipped list.

tasks.title: ARCHITECTURE NOTE: Confirmed cumulative branch chain — T094 design documentation and invariant enforcement
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_05_execution.js executeSplitAsync() in session 6 (line ~390): the critical line `currentBase = split.name` at the end of the branch creation loop. Each split initially checks out from `currentBase` (which starts as plan.baseBranch) and then becomes the new `currentBase` for the next split. This means: split[0] branches off baseBranch, split[1] branches off split[0], split[2] branches off split[1], etc. Splits form a CUMULATIVE CHAIN — each split contains ALL the files from all prior splits PLUS its own new files. This makes verifyEquivalence (T094) CORRECT: comparing lastSplit^{tree} vs sourceBranch^{tree} does correctly detect whether the full change set has been captured. Action items: (1) Add an explicit DESIGN NOTE comment in pr_split_05_execution.js and pr_split_06_verification.js: '// INVARIANT: Split branches form a cumulative chain. split[i] is based on split[i-1]. // The final split contains ALL files from all prior splits. // This is why verifyEquivalence only needs to compare the LAST split tree to source.' (2) Add an assertion/test in pr_split_06_verification_test.go confirming that for a known 3-split cumulative-chain plan, verifyEquivalence correctly reports equivalent=true. (3) Close T094 by confirming the EXISTING implementation is correct and only the documentation is missing. (4) Update T094 status to confirm the cumulative chain — the fix required is documentation only, not redesign.

tasks.title: resolveConflictsWithClaude runs resolution.commands via exec.execv (sync) — blocks Goja event loop
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_10_pipeline.js resolveConflictsWithClaude() in session 8 (lines ~1020-1025): after applying patches, the code runs each resolution.commands[c] via: exec.execv(['sh', '-c', 'cd ' + shellQuote(patchWorktreeDir) + ' && ' + resolution.commands[c]]). exec.execv is a BLOCKING synchronous call that freezes the entire Goja event loop for the duration of the shell command. If Claude suggests 'go mod tidy', 'npm install', 'cargo build', or any similar slow command, this blocks for 10-120+ seconds. During this freeze: (1) All tea.tick messages are queued but not processed, (2) The TUI becomes completely unresponsive, (3) No heartbeat from Claude can be processed, (4) No Ctrl+C/Ctrl+P can be detected. Then gitAddChangedFilesAsync (async) is called — but the damage is already done. This is distinct from T103 (worktree path fragility) — T109 is specifically about sync vs. async execution. Fix: (1) Replace exec.execv with exec.spawn wrapped in a Promise: `await new Promise(function(resolve) { exec.spawn(['sh', '-c', shellQuote(patchWorktreeDir) + ' && ' + cmd], { onExit: resolve }); })`. (2) Enforce a per-command timeout via a race with setTimeout Promise (default 60s, configurable via config.resolveCommandTimeoutMs). (3) Stream command stdout/stderr to emitOutput() for live TUI display of repair progress. (4) Write a unit test verifying that a 200ms resolution command does not block the event loop for more than 50ms on the calling thread.

tasks.title: automatedSplit never assigns state.analysisCache after Step 1 — resume re-split path uses empty file lists
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_01_analysis.js (session 8) and cross-referencing with pr_split_10_pipeline.js (session 7-8). analyzeDiffAsync() returns {files, fileStatuses, baseBranch, currentBranch} but does NOT populate prSplit._state.analysisCache. In automatedSplit: (1) state.analysisCache = null is reset at startup. (2) Step 1 calls analyzeDiffAsync and stores result in local var 'analysis'. (3) Nowhere in automatedSplit is state.analysisCache ever assigned from 'analysis'. (4) savePlan(null, 'Generate split plan') is called with state.analysisCache still null — the checkpoint serializes null to disk. (5) On resume path: analysis = state.analysisCache || { files: [], fileStatuses: {}, baseBranch: '', currentBranch: '' } — always falls back to the empty object. (6) If a re-split is triggered during a resumed pipeline (Step 9 re-classify/re-execute block), createSplitPlan() receives analysis.baseBranch='' and analysis.currentBranch='' — git operations fail with 'ambiguous argument' or 'unknown revision'. Impact: Only affects the re-split recovery path during resumed sessions. Normal resume (Steps 7-10) proceeds using plan.baseBranch directly without needing state.analysisCache. But re-split is a critical recovery path. Fix: After successful Step 1 completion (after the files.length===0 early-return check), add exactly one line: state.analysisCache = analysis. Write a unit test verifying savePlan writes a non-null analysisCache to disk and loadPlan restores it, and that a resumed re-split uses the correct baseBranch and currentBranch values.

tasks.title: renderClassificationPrompt silently sends empty string to Claude when osm:text/template unavailable
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_09_claude.js renderPrompt() and renderClassificationPrompt() in session 9: renderPrompt() returns { text: string, error: string|null }. When osm:text/template is unavailable, it returns { text: '', error: 'osm:text/template module not available' }. But renderClassificationPrompt(), renderSplitPlanPrompt(), and renderConflictPrompt() do NOT check renderPrompt().error — they propagate the result but callers don't inspect it. In automatedSplit() step 3, the code sends renderResult.text to Claude without verifying renderResult.error. If template rendering fails, Claude receives an empty string prompt, has zero context for classification, and produces undefined output or generic error. Fix: (1) In renderClassificationPrompt, renderSplitPlanPrompt, and renderConflictPrompt: ensure callers receive the error — add jsdoc annotations. (2) In automatedSplit() step 3 (sendToHandle pre-flight): add `if (renderResult.error) { return { error: 'Failed to render classification prompt: ' + renderResult.error }; }` before calling sendToHandle. (3) In resolveConflictsWithClaude: check renderConflictPrompt result.error before sending. (4) Write a unit test: mock prSplit._modules.template = null, assert renderClassificationPrompt returns { text: '', error: string } and automatedSplit returns an error at step 3 without calling sendToHandle.

tasks.title: detectLanguage returns 'unknown' for unrecognized file extensions — prompt renders 'a unknown project' (grammatical error, bad context)
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_09_claude.js detectLanguage() in session 9: when no recognized extension matches (e.g., .svelte, .vue, .sol, .tf, .nix), the function returns 'unknown'. The CLASSIFICATION_PROMPT_TEMPLATE uses 'The repository is a {{.Language}} project' — substituting 'unknown' produces 'The repository is a unknown project' which is: (a) grammatically wrong (should be 'an unknown'), (b) unhelpful to Claude. Fix: (1) Add to detectLanguage: if best === 'unknown', look at extension frequencies and return the top extension as a pseudo-language (e.g., '.svelte' files → 'Svelte'). (2) In CLASSIFICATION_PROMPT_TEMPLATE, change to: '{{if .Language}}The repository is a {{.Language}} project{{else}}The repository has {{.FileCount}} changed files{{end}}.' — this requires adding FileCount to the template data. (3) Change the 'a {{.Language}}' to handle articles properly: add helper in template: '{{if vowel .Language}}an{{else}}a{{end}} {{.Language}}' OR just change to 'a {{.Language}}' → 'The repository uses {{.Language}}'. Simplest fix: '{{if .Language}}The repository uses the {{.Language}} programming language{{end}}'. (4) Write a unit test: pass a list of .svelte files to renderClassificationPrompt, assert the rendered text does not contain 'a unknown' and DOES contain useful language context.

tasks.title: startAutoAnalysis calls synchronous isAvailable() from BubbleTea Update handler — blocks event loop when executor not yet resolved
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_16_tui_core.js startAutoAnalysis() in session 9: the code calls `st.claudeExecutor.isAvailable()` — the SYNCHRONOUS ClaudeCodeExecutor.prototype.isAvailable() which calls resolve() → exec.execv(['which', 'claude']) — a BLOCKING subprocess call. This runs in the BubbleTea Update handler (via handleNext → startAutoAnalysis when mode==='auto'). The synchronous version short-circuits (`if (this.resolved) return true;`) ONLY if handleClaudeCheck has already run and populated `st.claudeExecutor.resolved`. In the following edge cases, the blocking call DOES occur: (a) User selects 'auto' strategy and immediately presses Enter/Next before the auto-detect tick runs; (b) `st.claudeExecutor` is null and gets created fresh in startAutoAnalysis without prior resolution; (c) In tests or headless mode where handleClaudeCheck is never triggered. Fix: (1) Add a guard: if st.claudeExecutor is freshly created AND st.claudeExecutor.resolved === null, schedule `return [s, tea.tick(1, 'check-claude')];` to defer to HandleClaudeCheck. Set a pending flag `s.pendingAutoAnalysis = true`. (2) In handleClaudeCheckPoll when check completes and `s.pendingAutoAnalysis` is true, clear the flag and call `return startAutoAnalysis(s)`. (3) Document that startAutoAnalysis assumes claudeExecutor.resolved is already populated. (4) Write a TUI test ensuring startAutoAnalysis when called with an unresolved executor schedules check-claude instead of blocking.

tasks.title: handleRestartClaudePoll calls startAnalysis (heuristic) after successful Claude restart — user loses Claude-based workflow
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_16_tui_core.js handleRestartClaudePoll() in session 9 (end of function): after `claudeExecutor.restart()` succeeds, the handler unconditionally calls `return startAnalysis(s)`. `startAnalysis(s)` is the HEURISTIC grouping path that uses local file analysis only — it does NOT use Claude regardless of `prSplit.runtime.mode`. After a successful restart, the user sees 'Claude restarted successfully' in logs but the TUI then runs heuristic analysis, silently abandoning the Claude-based workflow. The user ends up with a heuristic plan they didn't ask for. Fix: (1) Change to mode-aware call: `if (prSplit.runtime.mode === 'auto') { return startAutoAnalysis(s); } return startAnalysis(s);`. (2) In startAutoAnalysis, if `st.planCache` already exists (plan was generated before the crash), skip to step 6 (execution) rather than re-running classification and planning. Add: `if (st.planCache && st.planCache.splits && st.planCache.splits.length > 0) { return startExecution(s); }` at the top of startAutoAnalysis. (3) Add a crash-recovery TUI notification: show a temporary badge 'Resumed after Claude crash' in the title bar for 5 seconds. (4) Write a test: simulate crash→restart with existing plan, assert startExecution is called not startAnalysis.

tasks.title: pollVerifySession (PTY-based path) hardcodes preExisting:false — pre-existing failures always treated as new on all Unix systems
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_16_tui_core.js pollVerifySession() in session 9: when the primary PTY-based CaptureSession verification completes, the result always has `preExisting: false` hardcoded. ONLY the async fallback path (runVerifyFallbackAsync, used when CaptureSession unavailable) correctly propagates `verifyResult.preExisting` from verifySplitAsync(). On every Unix system (macOS, Linux — all systems with PTY support), the primary PTY path is used — meaning preExisting is NEVER set to true via the primary path. The consequence: every pre-existing failure (one that also fails on the base branch) is treated as a new failure, triggering resolveConflictsWithClaude for failures that CAN'T be fixed by changing the split. This wastes AI API calls, adds latency, and generates misleading 'resolution' suggestions. Fix: (1) After the CaptureSession completes and exitCode !== 0, run a quick async check on the BASE branch: `var baseResult = await prSplit.verifySplitAsync(plan.baseBranch, {...})`. (2) Cache the base result (keyed by baseBranch) to avoid re-running for each split. (3) If base also fails: set `preExisting: true` in the result, add a gray 'pre-existing' badge in viewVerificationScreen. (4) In the error resolution screen, show '(this failure also exists on base branch — cannot fix by modifying split)'. (5) In resolveConflictsWithClaude, skip branches with preExisting:true unless config.resolvePreExisting is true. (6) Write a test: mock verify on base branch as failing, mock verify on split as failing, assert pollVerifySession produces preExisting:true.

tasks.title: handleAutoSplitPoll two error paths set s.wizardState='ERROR' without wizard.transition() — state machine diverges from render state
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_16_tui_core.js handleAutoSplitPoll() completion handler in session 9: two error paths exist: (1) `if (result && result.error) { s.errorDetails = result.error; s.wizardState = 'ERROR'; return [s, null]; }` and (2) the 'no plan or splits' else: `s.errorDetails = '...'; s.wizardState = 'ERROR'; return [s, null];` — NEITHER calls `s.wizard.transition('ERROR')`. After these paths, `s.wizard.current` (state machine's underlying state) is still CONFIG or whatever it was before, while `s.wizardState` says 'ERROR'. Functions that check `wizard.current` will behave incorrectly: handleFinalizationState returns error because wizard.current !== 'FINALIZATION'; handleBack hit the default case and does nothing; handleNext switch hits wrong case based on stale wizard.current. Fix: (1) Insert `try { s.wizard.transition('ERROR'); } catch (te) { /* not reachable from all states — force it */ }` before each `s.wizardState = 'ERROR'`. (2) If the transition fails (ErrorTransition), force-set s.wizardState to a displayable error state and log the inconsistency. (3) Audit ALL occurrences of `s.wizardState =` in the file to verify each has a corresponding `s.wizard.transition()` or uses `s.wizardState = s.wizard.current` after a transition. (4) Write a unit test: simulate pipeline result.error, assert s.wizard.current === s.wizardState === 'ERROR' after handleAutoSplitPoll.

tasks.title: executeSplitAsync and executeSplit only check isCancelled() — isForceCancelled() (double Ctrl+C) ignored, no immediate abort
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_05_execution.js (both executeSplit sync at lines 1-217 and executeSplitAsync at lines 218-420 in sessions 6-9): every cancellation check calls `prSplit.isCancelled()`. `prSplit.isForceCancelled()` is defined in pr_split_00_core.js (checks _cancelSource('forceCancelled')) but NEVER called in execution code. In the TUI, double-pressing Ctrl+C triggers the FORCE_CANCEL state and sets `_cancelSource` to return true for 'forceCancelled'. However, since executeSplit(Async) never checks this, force-cancel users experience identical slow cancellation — the loop continues until the next `isCancelled()` check point (potentially through another split or multiple files). Users expect immediate abort from force-cancel. Fix: (1) In executeSplit() and executeSplitAsync(): change each `if (prSplit.isCancelled())` to `if (prSplit.isCancelled() || prSplit.isForceCancelled())`. For force-cancel specifically: `if (prSplit.isForceCancelled()) { cleanupWorktreeIfSafe(); return { error: 'force-cancelled by user', results: results }; }` — consider skipping worktree cleanup on force-cancel to abort faster. (2) Similarly for startVerifySession / pollVerifySession: kill the CaptureSession immediately when isForceCancelled() returns true (currently only isCancelled() is checked before stopping poll). (3) Write a unit test: set _cancelSource to return true for 'forceCancelled', call executeSplitAsync with 5 splits, assert it returns after 0 splits processed (no splits completed).

tasks.title: viewVerificationScreen (EQUIV_CHECK) renders equiv.expected/equiv.actual which are always undefined — tree hashes always blank on FAIL
viewPlanReviewScreen 'Ask Claude' button uses primaryButton style (unfocused) while Edit/Regen use secondaryButton — layout shift on Tab cycle
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_15_tui_views.js viewPlanReviewScreen() in session 10: the three action buttons at the bottom of the PLAN_REVIEW screen are rendered as: `var editBtnStyle = editFocused ? styles.focusedButton() : styles.secondaryButton()` and `var regenBtnStyle = regenFocused ? styles.focusedButton() : styles.secondaryButton()` — BUT `var askClaudeStyle = askClaudeFocused ? styles.focusedButton() : styles.primaryButton()`. The Ask Claude button uses `primaryButton` when NOT focused, unlike the Edit and Regenerate buttons which use `secondaryButton`. Since primaryButton has different border/padding than secondaryButton (root cause: T011), when the user Tab-cycles into or out of the Ask Claude button, the horizontal row shifts — Edit and Regenerate buttons move because the row's total width changes. This is the same T011 root cause as T062 (finalization) and T063 (error resolution) but on the PLAN_REVIEW screen. Fix: Change `askClaudeStyle = askClaudeFocused ? styles.focusedButton() : styles.primaryButton()` to `askClaudeStyle = askClaudeFocused ? styles.focusedButton() : styles.secondaryButton()`. Visually, Ask Claude can still be distinguished by its emoji (🤖) or by wrapping it in a zone mark with a different background. After T011 is resolved (uniform `focusedSecondaryButton` style), this fix becomes redundant — include as part of T011's sweep. File: pr_split_15_tui_views.js viewPlanReviewScreen(). Write a test confirming all three button styles are `secondaryButton`-width when unfocused.

tasks.title: _viewFn (BubbleTea View) mutates s._isNavNextFocused and calls s.vp.setWidth/setHeight — state mutations inside pure View function
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_16_tui_core.js _viewFn (createWizardModel closure) in session 10, lines ~1005-1035. The BubbleTea View function (`_viewFn`) which must be a pure function performs two categories of state mutation: (1) Derived state: `s._isNavNextFocused = false; ... s._isNavNextFocused = true;` — this computes the nav-next focus flag BEFORE calling renderNavBar(s) so that renderNavBar can read it from `s`. This is a benign derived value, but mutating `s` inside the View violates BubbleTea's model immutability contract. (2) Viewport resize: `s.vp.setWidth(w); s.vp.setHeight(vpHeight);` — this resizes the main content viewport (a bubbletea.Viewport component) inside the View function. The Viewport component itself tracks scroll state; resizing it during View can cause double-render artifacts if BubbleTea calls View multiple times or if scroll position is adjusted as a side effect. This is the same pattern as T080 (viewReportOverlay) and T028 (renderStatusBar) but on the central layout viewport. Fix: (1) REMOVE `s._isNavNextFocused` mutation from _viewFn. Instead, compute the flag locally in renderNavBar: `var focusElems = getFocusElements(s); var isNavNextFocused = (focusElems.length > 0 && (s.focusIndex||0) === focusElems.length-1 && focusElems[focusElems.length-1].type==='nav'); var nextBtnStyle = isNavNextFocused ? styles.focusedButton() : styles.primaryButton()`. (2) MOVE `s.vp.setWidth/setHeight` calls to the WindowResize handler in `_updateFn`: `if (msg.type === 'WindowSize') { s._viewportW = msg.width; s._viewportH = msg.height; ... ; if (s.vp) { s.vp.setWidth(vpW); s.vp.setHeight(vpH); } }`. Cache last-rendered dimensions in `s._lastW` and `s._lastH`. (3) Write a test verifying _viewFn can be called twice consecutively without changing the model's focusIndex or viewport dimensions.

tasks.title: automatedSplit never sets report.equivalence — user TRAPPED at BRANCH_BUILDING state after automated split, cannot reach FINALIZATION
tasks.description: CONFIRMED by reading pr_split_10_pipeline.js automatedSplit() (session 12) and cross-referencing TUI code (pr_split_16_tui_core.js lines 2928, 2946-2960, handleNext lines 2025-2068). SEVERITY: CRITICAL — the user is permanently trapped at BRANCH_BUILDING state after automated split. ROOT CAUSE: automatedSplit Step 10 captures equivalence locally: `var equivResult = await step('Verify equivalence', ...)` — `equivResult.result` has the full equivalence data but `report.equivalence` is NEVER assigned. In handleAutoSplitPoll line 2946: `if (report.equivalence) { s.wizard.transition('BRANCH_BUILDING'); s.wizard.transition('EQUIV_CHECK'); }` — since report.equivalence is always undefined/falsy, this branch is NEVER taken. The ELSE branch (line 2954) always executes: `s.wizard.transition('BRANCH_BUILDING'); s.wizardState = 'BRANCH_BUILDING'`. Additionally, handleNext() (line 2025) has NO case for 'BRANCH_BUILDING' — falls through to `default: return [s, null]`. So pressing Enter from BRANCH_BUILDING does NOTHING. No tick auto-advances from BRANCH_BUILDING to EQUIV_CHECK in the automated path. Therefore: (1) After automated split completes, wizard is permanently stuck at BRANCH_BUILDING. (2) User sees the execution progress screen (all branches ✓) with no path forward. (3) The 'Done', 'Create PRs', and 'View Report' buttons of FINALIZATION screen NEVER appear. (4) The equivalence check ran internally in the pipeline but neither the result nor the transition was propagated. FIXES REQUIRED: (1) In automatedSplit, after Step 10: add `report.equivalence = equivResult && equivResult.result ? equivResult.result : null;`. (2) Also fix heuristicFallback: add `report.equivalence = equiv;` before the error assignment. (3) Add a 'BRANCH_BUILDING' case to handleNext that calls startEquivCheck(s) when the automated split has completed — this provides the fallback for users who somehow reach BRANCH_BUILDING without the auto-advancement. (4) Tests: assert report.equivalence is set after automatedSplit; assert that handleAutoSplitPoll transitions to EQUIV_CHECK when report.equivalence is truthy; assert handleNext from BRANCH_BUILDING calls startEquivCheck.

tasks.description: CONFIRMED by cross-referencing pr_split_10_pipeline.js (mcpCallbackObj.addTool schema, lines 1321-1340) with pr_split_16_tui_core.js processClaudeConvoResult (line 4162) and formatClaudeResponse (line 4143) in session 12.
